body_full:
Dear Participant,

This is a *final reminder* to complete your payment for participating in
*Techkriti*.

If you have already completed the payment, please *ignore this email*.

For those who have not yet completed the payment, kindly do so *before the
deadline: 14th March* to confirm your participation.

You can complete your payment using the link below:
*Payment Link:* Click Here
<https://onlinesbi.sbi.bank.in/sbicollect/icollecthome.htm?corpID=314456>

Website: techkriti.org

After completing the payment, please make sure to *download the receipt and
upload it on the portal* as required.

If you face any issues or have any queries, feel free to reach out to us.

We look forward to seeing you at *Techkriti!*

Best regards,


-- 

[image: Techkriti_logo.png]
*KHUSHBOO KUMARI*
Head Events & Competitions | Techkriti'26
Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur
*Email:* khushboo@techkriti.org <hospitality@techkriti.org>
*Phone*: (+91) 9352941607

*Join Us:* Website <http://www.techkriti.org/> | Facebook
<https://www.facebook.com/techkriti.iitk> | Twitter
<https://twitter.com/techkriti_iitk> | Google+
<http://google.com/+TechkritiOrg_iitk> | Instagram
<https://www.instagram.com/techkriti.iitk/> | YouTube
<https://www.youtube.com/user/TechkritiIITK> | LinkedIn
<https://www.linkedin.com/company/techkriti%2713>

subject: Re: Permission for Drone Fly in CU-UP campus
body_preview:
Dear Sir,

I would like to clarify the regulatory aspects regarding the drone we
intend to fly for practice for the upcoming Multirotor drone racing
competition at Techkriti (IIT Kanpur).

Our drone i
body_full:
Dear Sir,

I would like to clarify the regulatory aspects regarding the drone we
intend to fly for practice for the upcoming Multirotor drone racing
competition at Techkriti (IIT Kanpur).

Our drone is a *5-inch FPV drone that weighs under 250 grams*, which places
it in the *Nano drone category* as per the *DGCA Drone Rules 2021*.

Based on the regulations:

   1.

   *DGCA / Digital Sky Registration*
   Nano drones (below 250 g) used for recreational or research purposes *do
   not require a UIN registration* on the Digital Sky platform.
   2.

   *Remote Pilot Certificate*
   A Remote Pilot Certificate is *not required for nano drones* under 250 g.
   3.

   *Airspace Zone*
   The university campus area falls under a *Green Zone*, where drone
   operations *below 120 meters are permitted without prior DGCA airspace
   permission*.
   4.

   *Flight Conditions We Will Follow*
   -

      The drone will be flown *Line-of-Sight (LoS)* only.
      -

      Maximum altitude will be *well below 20 meters*.
      -

      Flights will take place *beside the basketball court*, away from
      crowds and buildings.
      -

      Strict safety precautions will be followed during all practice
      sessions.

Considering that the drone falls in the *nano category*, the *location is
within a green zone*, and the *flight altitude will be very low*, the
activity remains *safe and compliant with DGCA guidelines*.

Therefore, I kindly request you to *grant us permission to practice flying
our drone in the designated area beside the basketball court* so that we
can properly prepare for the competition.

Thank you for your time and consideration.

Yours sincerely,
Sujal Negi
Student, School of Computer Science and Engineering
Chandigarh University

>
>
> To fly a 5-inch FPV drone on a university campus in India, we must follow
> the rules under the Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) Drone
> Rules 2021. Even if we fly line-of-sight and below 20 m, permissions may
> still be required depending on the drone category and airspace zone.
>
> Below is the practical list of permissions we may need.
>
> 1. *D

subject: [GitHub Education] 💖 @sujalnegi
body_preview:
Hey Sujal Negi,

Welcome!

Thank you for joining https://education.github.com/globalcampus/student?email_referrer=true. GitHub Education helps students, teachers, and schools access the tools and reso
body_full:
Hey Sujal Negi,

Welcome!

Thank you for joining https://education.github.com/globalcampus/student?email_referrer=true. GitHub Education helps students, teachers, and schools access the tools and resources they need to shape the next generation of software development.


  Congratulations, you are now a GitHub Education student! You can now explore valuable offers provided by GitHub's partners in the GitHub Student Developer Pack, view student events, and much more when you sign in at:

    https://education.github.com/globalcampus/student?email_referrer=true

  If you are renewing your membership please be aware that some of our partner offers are single-use and non-renewable, and that you should contact the partner directly if you have any questions.


  Learn about GitHub Education's programs for students, like GitHub Campus Experts and our grants for first-time hackathons:
    https://education.github.com/students

  Introduce yourself to the GitHub Education Community:
    https://github.com/orgs/github-community/discussions/categories/github-education

  Your teacher or faculty advisor can request private repositories for your class projects or student club. Send them to:
    https://help.github.com/categories/teaching-and-learning-with-github-education

  Give the gift of GitHub Education to your entire school:
    https://education.github.com/partners/schools

  Have an Octotastic day!
  - The GitHub Education Team
